festivals.name: Istanbul International Music Festival
festivals.description: World-class classical, jazz, and world music performed in Istanbul's historic venues including Hagia Irene Church.

festivals.name: Whirling Dervishes of Konya (Şeb-i Arus)
festivals.description: Rumi's death anniversary brings thousands to Konya for the most important Sufi whirling ceremony in the world.

destinations.name: Istanbul
destinations.description: The only city straddling two continents has the Hagia Sophia, Blue Mosque, Grand Bazaar, and the Bosphorus strait.

destinations.name: Cappadocia
destinations.description: A volcanic dreamscape of fairy chimneys and cave churches, best experienced from a hot air balloon at dawn.

destinations.name: Ephesus
destinations.description: The best-preserved ancient Roman city in the Eastern Mediterranean, home to the magnificent Library of Celsus.

destinations.name: Pamukkale
destinations.description: Snow-white calcium terraces cascade down a hillside above the ancient spa city of Hierapolis.

destinations.name: Nemrut Dağı
destinations.description: Giant stone heads of gods and a king stare across the Anatolian landscape from a 2,000-year-old royal tomb at 2,150m.

destinations.name: Konya
destinations.description: The spiritual heart of Sufi Islam where Rumi's tomb draws pilgrims and the whirling dervish ceremony is performed.

destinations.name: Trabzon & Sumela Monastery
destinations.description: A Byzantine monastery clinging to a sheer cliff face in misty green mountains above the Black Sea coast.

destinations.name: Göbekli Tepe
destinations.description: The world's oldest temple complex at 11,500 years old rewrites human history — built thousands of years before Stonehenge.
